meta.title: Political Cycle Correlation with Public Works Contract Awards
meta.methodology: Aggregated PublicContract fecha_perfeccionamiento and PublicWork start_date by year and quarter, cross-referenced with Argentine presidential election years (2015, 2019, 2023) and term boundaries. Contractor continuity analyzed across administrations.
meta.hypothesis: Contract awards spike before elections and at the start/end of presidential terms

political_cycle_patterns.description: Q2 consistently has the highest contract volume across all years (2015-2022). This aligns with Argentina's fiscal cycle: budget approved in Q4/Q1, procurement processes initiated in Q1, contracts perfected in Q2. This is a fiscal-bureaucratic pattern, not directly political.

Public works started jumped from 149 (2019) to 668 (2020) to 2,740 (2021) to 1,968 (2022), an 18x increase in 2 years. Contracts similarly exploded from 373 (2021) to 1,357 (2022 H1 only). This massive acceleration ahead of the 2023 election is the strongest political cycle signal in the data.

Only 24 contracts were signed in 2019 Q4 but totaling $70.4M — average $2.9M per contract vs $178K avg in Q2 of the same year. This coincides with the lame-duck period between Macri's election loss (Oct 27) and Fernandez inauguration (Dec 10). High-value last-minute awards during transition.
Contract volume dropped from 34 (Q3) to 6 (Q4) in the 2015 election year, consistent with transition freeze as Macri took over from CFK.
Public works started collapsed from 1,393 (2023) to 18 (2024), a 99% reduction, confirming the new administration's fiscal austerity policy and abrupt halt of the previous cycle's spending spree.

contractor_favoritism_indicators.finding: Received $360M in 9 contracts under Alberto Fernandez (avg $40M each) vs negligible amounts under Macri and Kirchnerismo. This 12,000x value increase between administrations is the most extreme favoritism signal in the dataset.

Consistent presence across Macri and Alberto Fernandez, but value jumped 10x from $14M (Macri, 19 contracts) to $138M (AlbertoF, 27 contracts). The contract count only increased 42% but value increased 915%, suggesting inflated contract values under Fernandez.
Present across 3 administrations but 98% of $132M value concentrated under Alberto Fernandez. Volume also concentrated: 32 of 55 total contracts under one administration.
Single contract worth $97.8M under Alberto Fernandez. Single large-value contracts are a common procurement fraud vector.

findings.sector_observation: Top contractors are overwhelmingly pharmaceutical/medical supply companies (BIOARS, DROGUERIA ARTIGAS, BIODIAGNOSTICO, FRESENIUS KABI, BECTON DICKINSON, BIOMÉRIEUX). The COVID-19 pandemic (2020-2021) likely amplified medical procurement but does not fully explain the favoritism patterns — BIOARS's $40M average contract value far exceeds industry norms.
limitations: Contract data appears to start around 2014, limiting pre-2015 election cycle analysis
2022 data ends at Q2, making full-year comparison impossible
Amounts are in nominal ARS — high inflation distorts year-over-year comparisons without deflation
PublicWork start_date values cluster in Q4, possibly due to data recording conventions rather than actual start dates
COVID-19 pandemic (2020-2021) is a confounding variable for the spending acceleration pattern
